examples.title: Reading Temperature Sensor via GPIO
examples.description: Python code to read temperature from a DS18B20 sensor connected to Raspberry Pi GPIO
examples.code:
import os
import time
from w1thermsensor import W1ThermSensor

# Initialize the sensor
sensor = W1ThermSensor()

def read_temperature():
 # Read temperature in Celsius
 temperature = sensor.get_temperature()
 return temperature

# Main loop - reads temperature every 5 seconds
try:
 while True:
 temp = read_temperature()
 print(f"Temperature: {temp:.2f}°C")
 time.sleep(5)
except KeyboardInterrupt:
 print("\nMonitoring stopped")
examples.explanation: This code reads temperature data from a digital temperature sensor (DS18B20) connected via GPIO pins. The sensor uses the 1-Wire protocol to communicate with the Raspberry Pi.

examples.title: GPIO LED Control with Button Input
examples.description: Python code to control an LED using a push button via Raspberry Pi GPIO
examples.code:
import RPi.GPIO as GPIO
import time

# Pin configuration
LED_PIN = 18
BUTTON_PIN = 17

# Setup GPIO mode
GPIO.setmode(GPIO.BCM)
GPIO.setup(LED_PIN, GPIO.OUT)
GPIO.setup(BUTTON_PIN, GPIO.IN, pull_up_down=GPIO.PUD_UP)

try:
 while True:
 # Read button state (pressed = LOW due to pull-up)
 if GPIO.input(BUTTON_PIN) == GPIO.LOW:
 GPIO.output(LED_PIN, GPIO.HIGH)
 print("Button pressed - LED ON")
 else:
 GPIO.output(LED_PIN, GPIO.LOW)
 print("Button released - LED OFF")
 time.sleep(0.1)
except KeyboardInterrupt:
 GPIO.cleanup()
 print("Program terminated")
examples.explanation: This code demonstrates basic GPIO input/output. A push button is connected as input, and an LED as output. When the button is pressed, the LED turns on, demonstrating physical world interaction.

examples.title: IoT Data Publishing to Cloud
examples.description: Python code to publish sensor data to MQTT cloud broker from Raspberry Pi
examples.code:
import paho.mqtt.client as mqtt
import json
import time
import random

# MQTT Broker configuration
BROKER = "test.mosquitto.org"
PORT = 1883
TOPIC = "iot/sensor/temperature"

# Callback when connection is established
def on_connect(client, userdata, flags, rc):
 if rc == 0:
 print("Connected to MQTT Broker!")
 else:
 print(f"Connection failed with code {rc}")

# Create client and set callback
client = mqtt.Client()
client.on_connect = on_connect

# Connect to broker
client.connect(BROKER, PORT, 60)

# Start the loop
client.loop_start()

# Simulate and publish sensor data
try:
 while True:
 # Simulate temperature reading
 temperature = random.uniform(20.0, 30.0)
 humidity = random.uniform(40.0, 60.0)
 
 # Create JSON payload
 payload = json.dumps({
 "device": "RaspberryPi_01",
 "temperature": round(temperature, 2),
 "humidity": round(humidity, 2),
 "timestamp": time.time()
 })
 
 # Publish to topic
 client.publish(TOPIC, payload)
 print(f"Published: {payload}")
 
 time.sleep(10)
except KeyboardInterrupt:
 client.loop_stop()
 client.disconnect()
 print("Disconnected from broker")
examples.explanation: This code demonstrates IoT data transmission using MQTT protocol. It simulates reading temperature and humidity sensors, packages the data as JSON, and publishes it to a cloud MQTT broker for remote monitoring.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is number of publications
